name: Roasted Corn Soup
recipeIngredient: 4 Cups chicken stock or 4 tsp chicken stock powder in 4 cups water.
recipeInstructions:
1. Pull back the husks from the corn cobs and remove the silk.
2. Melt the butter and brush over the kernels, then pull the husks back over to cover them again.
3. Place the corn in one layer in a roasting pan with whole, unpeeled garlic cloves and halved red capsicum (if using), and bake at 220°C for 20 minutes.
4. While the corn is roasting, peel and roughly chop the potatoes. Cook them in a large pot with chicken stock until tender.
5. Using a slotted spoon, lift out the potatoes and put them in a food processor, blender, or mouli to puree.
6. Remove the husks from the roasted cobs.
7. Cut the corn from the cobs using a sharp serrated knife.
8. Scrape any remaining corn flesh from the cobs with a spoon.
9. Puree the kernels and flesh from three of the cobs, adding the pulp squeezed from the garlic cloves.
10. Return the mixture to the pot with the chicken stock.
11. Finely dice the capsicum (discard the stem and seeds).
12. Add the diced capsicum, corn cut from the last cob, cream, and bring the soup to a boil.
13. Adjust seasonings to taste, adding salt, sugar, Tabasco sauce, and freshly ground black pepper if necessary.
14. Reheat just before serving.
15. Top each serving with finely chopped parsley.
